title: An autonomous laboratory for the accelerated synthesis of inorganic materials
project_lesson: Closed loops need computation, literature-derived priors, ML/active learning, robotic execution, and failure analysis as separate auditable layers.

title: Autonomous chemical research with large language models
project_lesson: Autonomous experiment planning must expose tool use, planning, execution, and human-auditable steps; do not hide experimental decisions inside one opaque LLM call.

title: Augmenting large language models with chemistry tools
project_lesson: Domain tools should be explicit modules behind an LLM planner; CultivateAgent should keep deterministic normalization/evidence tools outside the LLM.

title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
project_lesson: Useful for staged ideation/execution/review structure, but too autonomous for wet-lab claims; keep human gates before experiments.

title: Language agents achieve superhuman synthesis of scientific knowledge
project_lesson: Use full-text retrieval, citation grounding, query refinement, and contradiction checks; abstract-only extraction is insufficient.

title: Synthesizing scientific literature with retrieval-augmented language models
project_lesson: Specialized scientific RAG benefits from large paper stores, adaptive retrieval, and self-feedback; evidence citation accuracy should be measured.

title: LAB-Bench: Language Agent Biology Benchmark
project_lesson: Use biology-specific evaluation tasks and report accuracy/coverage; CultivateAgent should benchmark extraction/review tasks, not only demo outputs.

title: Structured information extraction from scientific text with large language models
project_lesson: Structured JSON-style extraction and task-specific evaluation are appropriate; broad schemas should be decomposed into field/section-specific jobs.

title: Large Language Models for Scientific Information Extraction: An Empirical Study for Virology
project_lesson: Scientific IE can be framed as structured contribution summaries; smaller fine-tuned models may compete on narrow IE after enough annotations.

title: GROBID: a machine learning software for extracting information from scholarly documents
project_lesson: PDFs should be converted into structured TEI/section/table objects where possible; plain fulltext.txt is too weak for reliable extraction.

title: S2ORC: The Semantic Scholar Open Research Corpus
project_lesson: Structured full text with citations, figures, and tables linked to paper objects is the target data model to emulate locally.

title: DocETL: Agentic Query Rewriting and Evaluation for Complex Document Processing
project_lesson: LLM document processing should be modular, schema-bound, and evaluated operator-by-operator rather than one monolithic prompt.

title: Scalable Global Optimization via Local Bayesian Optimization
project_lesson: Trust-region BO is a later upgrade when medium search space grows high-dimensional; not the immediate S3 bottleneck.

title: Scalable Constrained Bayesian Optimization
project_lesson: Useful later for hard constraints such as cost, animal-origin, osmolality, or food-grade bounds.

title: Joint Entropy Search for Multi-objective Bayesian Optimization
project_lesson: Information-theoretic MOBO is a later candidate when learning the Pareto set matters more than short-term hypervolume.

title: Diversified Sampling for Batched Bayesian Optimization with Determinantal Point Processes
project_lesson: Batch diversity is useful once wet-lab batches risk near-duplicate formulations.

title: Understanding the output (TEI)
project_lesson: GROBID uses TEI XML for structured document extraction; full-text output includes body div/head/p structures and figures, with tables commonly represented as figure type table.

title: GROBID Service API
project_lesson: GROBID recommends the web-service mode for production and benchmarking; CultivateAgent should call processFulltextDocument optionally, save returned TEI, and keep the plain-text fallback when the service is unavailable.

title: SchemaRAG: Dynamic Large Schema Reduction for LLM-driven Structured Information Extraction
project_lesson: Large fixed schemas hurt LLM IE; reduce/route the schema per document. Directly motivates decomposing the A-M schema into small per-document operator subsets rather than one monolithic prompt.

title: Effective and Efficient Schema-aware Information Extraction Using On-Device Large Language Models
project_lesson: Smaller focused schema contexts improve IE reliability; supports operator decomposition and section routing.

title: Meta-analysis in clinical trials (DerSimonian-Laird random-effects)
project_lesson: Closed-form random-effects pooling of heterogeneous effect sizes with between-study variance tau^2. Core method for the cross-paper evidence layer (no MCMC).

title: Quantifying heterogeneity in a meta-analysis (I^2)
project_lesson: I^2 quantifies between-study heterogeneity; used to flag context-dependent components that need direct experiments rather than pooled belief.

title: Weakly informative priors for the heterogeneity parameter in Bayesian random-effects meta-analysis
project_lesson: Guides the tau (heterogeneity) prior for a Bayesian random-effects variant; informs uncertainty widening under sparse/conflicting evidence.

title: Multi-objective Bayesian algorithm automatically discovers low-cost high-growth serum-free media for cellular agriculture
project_lesson: DIRECT PRIOR ART: MOBO already discovers serum-free media (C2C12). CultivateAgent must differentiate: literature-grounded space+priors, LLM evidence-cited proposer, and traceability vs pure BO.

title: piBO: Augmenting Acquisition Functions with User Beliefs for Bayesian Optimization
project_lesson: Mechanism for injecting a prior over promising regions into the acquisition, decaying as data accrues. CultivateAgent uses it to fold evidence-synthesis posteriors into the MOBO.
